workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Ryan Kowalski at Wide World Importers just sent this. Help me build the narrative.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-03-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into competitive positioning.

[00:08:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:16:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:27:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:31:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:33:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-N trap (Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output ); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-N failure mode (Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output )
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-N -- Separation of customer-facing draft from internal brief when one unified output
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
